text: Working with APIs allows different software systems to communicate with each other. When you make an HTTP request to an API, you typically receive a response in JSON format, which is easy for computers to read and process. Many APIs require authentication, often using an API key, to ensure only authorised users can access the data. It is also important to be aware of rate limits, which restrict how many requests you can make in a certain period. Finally, good error handling is essential, as APIs may return errors due to network issues or incorrect parameters, and you should always check the status code of each response.
